prevalence.value: 5.3% (BMI ≥30 kg/m²; note: for Asian populations, WHO recommends lower cutoff of BMI ≥25 kg/m² for obesity, yielding ~21-24% prevalence)
prevalence.population: Indian adults (~1.4 billion total population, ~70% adults)

incidence.value: Annual incidence ~1-2% among adults (new cases of obesity onset)
incidence.source: Estimated from NFHS trends and ICMR reports

burden.dalyPerCapita: ~150 DALYs per 100,000 population attributable to obesity
burden.mortalityRate: Obesity-attributable mortality ~4-5% of total CVD deaths (~0.5% crude rate)
burden.source: Global Burden of Disease Study 2019 (IHME) / ICMR 2023

riskFactors: High-calorie diet rich in refined carbs and fats
Genetic predisposition (e.g., thrifty gene hypothesis in South Asians)
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: BMI ≥30 kg/m² (WHO standard); for Asians, adjusted criteria: overweight BMI ≥23, obesity BMI ≥25 kg/m² (WHO 2004 expert consultation)

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: Low (~20-30% in urban areas via routine health checks; minimal in rural)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: ~10-15% achieve sustained weight loss through interventions

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: ICMR recommends Asian-specific BMI cutoffs (obesity ≥25 kg/m²) alongside waist circumference (>90 cm men, >80 cm women) for abdominal obesity
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Lifestyle modification (diet, exercise) per ICMR guidelines; pharmacotherapy (e.g., orlistat) and bariatric surgery in severe cases; integrated with NCD programs like NPCDCS
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Underdiagnosis in rural areas due to limited access; cultural stigma and preference for traditional diets; high glycemic load in Indian cuisine exacerbating risk
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: National programs like POSHAN Abhiyaan for nutrition; increasing telemedicine for urban-rural outreach; growing research via ICMR on South Asian phenotypes
